prompt.task: Generate comprehensive API documentation for the endpoint described below.
prompt.context: This documentation will be included in a developer portal used by third-party integrators. The audience ranges from junior developers to senior architects. Documentation should follow OpenAPI-style conventions where applicable.

constraints.constraint: Include request and response examples with realistic data.
Document all possible HTTP status codes and their meanings.
Note any rate limits or usage restrictions.
Use consistent formatting for parameter tables.

prompt.input:
Endpoint: POST /api/v2/orders Purpose: Create a new order in the system Auth: Bearer token (OAuth 2.0) Rate limit: 100 requests per minute
Required fields: - customer_id (string, UUID) - items (array of objects with product_id, quantity, unit_price) - shipping_address (object with street, city, state, postal_code, country)
Optional fields: - notes (string, max 500 chars) - priority (enum: standard, express, overnight) - discount_code (string)
Returns: Order object with generated order_id, status, estimated_delivery
prompt.output_format: Return documentation in markdown with these sections: 1. Endpoint Overview (method, path, description) 2. Authentication 3. Request Parameters (table format) 4. Request Body Schema 5. Example Request 6. Response Schema 7. Example Response 8. Error Codes (table format) 9. Rate Limiting No preamble. Start directly with the first heading.

example.output: | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |-----------|------|----------|-------------| | customer_id | string (UUID) | Yes | Unique customer identifier |

checks.check: All required fields from input are documented.
All mentioned status codes have descriptions.
